daily_brief: Escalating tensions in the Persian Gulf dominated headlines as the US launched strikes on Iran after Tehran attacked a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz and declared the waterway closed [BBC, France 24, Globe and Mail]. Iran subsequently struck targets in the UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain, with the IRGC warning of a 'crushing response' to further US aggression [Al Jazeera]. The crisis comes days after President Trump suggested interim negotiations were 'over.' In Washington, the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham at 71 sent shockwaves through the political establishment [France 24, BBC, RNZ]. Graham, who had just returned from Kyiv meeting President Zelensky, was a leading voice on foreign policy and chairman of the Senate Budget Committee. In Asia, Typhoon Bavi made landfall in eastern China after forcing nearly two million evacuations across Zhejiang province [BBC, France 24]. Southern Europe continued to battle extreme heat, with Spain reporting at least 12 deaths in one of its deadliest wildfires near Almería, while France's Eiffel Tower and Louvre closed early amid a severe heatwave [BBC Europe, RFI]. At the World Cup, Argentina defeated Switzerland 3-1 in extra time to advance to the semifinals [France 24].

top_stories.headline: US Launches Strikes on Iran After Ship Attack; Tehran Closes Strait of Hormuz
bullets: Iran's IRGC attacked a Cyprus-flagged container ship (MV GFS Galaxy) in the Strait of Hormuz, setting it ablaze and forcing crew evacuation; one Indian national remains missing
US Central Command responded with strikes on 140 Iranian military targets including missile sites, drone facilities, and communication networks
Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and launched retaliatory strikes against US bases and Gulf Arab states including UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain
The escalation follows Trump's suggestion that interim Iran deal negotiations were 'over' and marks the third round of US-Iran strikes this week

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized closure of strait and scale of US response

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided live blog coverage with emphasis on regional spillover to Oman

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Contextualized within broader energy crisis and failed negotiations

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted IRGC's vow of 'crushing response' and framed US actions as aggression

top_stories.headline: US Senator Lindsey Graham Dies at 71 After Sudden Illness
bullets: Republican Senator Lindsey Graham of South Carolina died Saturday evening from a 'brief and sudden illness' according to his office
Graham, 71, had just returned from Kyiv where he met President Zelensky on Friday to work on Russia sanctions legislation
He served as chairman of the Senate Budget Committee and was one of Washington's most influential foreign policy voices
Graham was a strong supporter of Israel and advocate for US support to Ukraine; Israeli President Herzog called him 'a beacon of moral clarity'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized his role as Trump ally and Israel supporter

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Noted his recent Ukraine trip and evolution from Trump critic to supporter

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Focused on his defense policy positions

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Headline emphasized his role as 'one of Israel's strongest US Senate allies'

top_stories.headline: Typhoon Bavi Makes Landfall in Eastern China, Nearly 2 Million Evacuated
bullets: Typhoon Bavi made landfall in Zhejiang province Saturday evening with winds of 144 km/h before weakening to a severe tropical storm
Nearly 1.7 million people were evacuated in Zhejiang with thousands more in neighboring provinces
The storm previously killed at least 17 people in the Philippines and left tens of thousands without power in Taiwan and Japan
China had already experienced deadly storms in the past week leaving 39 dead and causing reservoir failures

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized scale of evacuations and prior week's flooding deaths

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Noted storm's size comparable to width of France; detailed regional impact

top_stories.headline: Spain Battles One of Its Deadliest Wildfires as Heat Grips Southern Europe
bullets: At least 12 people killed in wildfire near Bedar in Almería region, including four Britons; 23 people still missing
Fire has burned 6,600 hectares; cause attributed to a fallen power line though electricity companies deny responsibility
France experiencing its third heatwave since May, with 24 departments under maximum red alert and temperatures reaching 40°C
Eiffel Tower, Louvre, and Musée d'Orsay closing early; several towns cancelling Bastille Day fireworks

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Focused on British casualties and search operations

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized impact on Paris tourism and national holiday events

top_stories.headline: Trump Administration Subpoenas New York Times Reporters Over Air Force One Report
bullets: Department of Justice subpoenaed multiple NYT journalists after they reported on security concerns with the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One
Federal agents delivered subpoenas to reporters' homes; journalists ordered to testify before federal grand jury in Manhattan
Subpoenas issued after FBI Director Kash Patel and DOJ officials met at White House on Friday
Trump used older Air Force One model to leave NATO summit in Turkey, citing threats from Iran

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Described it as 'dramatic escalation' of Trump's campaign against media; emphasized erosion of press freedom

additional_stories.headline: Argentina defeats Switzerland 3-1 in extra time to reach World Cup semifinals, setting up clash with England
England beats Norway 2-1 in extra time on Bellingham brace to advance to World Cup semifinals
At least two killed in shooting at Toronto salsa festival; police say gunfire exchanged between two individuals
Polish PM Tusk announces national memorial to victims of WWII 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists,' urging Ukraine to embrace truth for EU membership
Germany agrees deal to buy US Tomahawk missiles as stopgap while Europe develops own defense systems
Police arrest 28-year-old suspect in killing of UK politician
Man nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane window mid-flight after it dislodged; passenger rescued by wife
Linda Noskova defeats Karolina Muchova in dramatic all-Czech Wimbledon final after recovering from five missed championship points
Russia strikes kill eight across Ukraine including five in Sumy; Zelensky calls for faster weapons deliveries
Nigeria says army killed over 300 bandits in two-day operation in Zamfara state
